definition: I2S reconstructs URI scheme delimiter bytes (colon and slashes for http://) from logged CmpLogObserver operands, forming a valid scheme prefix that enables a nonzero host-parse return.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads logged parser delimiter operands (':', '/', '/') from CmpLogObserver and substitutes them at matching input offsets, reconstructing the 'http://' scheme prefix that drives xmlParse3986Host to a nonzero return.
signature.one_line: I2S reconstructs 'http://[' scheme prefix enabling nonzero host-parse return

analysis.summary_one_line: An input containing a literal 'http://' scheme-and-authority prefix so xmlParse3986Host runs on the URI and returns nonzero, taking the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte sequence reconstructing the exact 'http://' scheme delimiter (':', '/', '/') at offsets ~0x05-0x08 followed by an authority body, so that xmlParse3986HierPart sees '//' at the cursor (line 772) and invokes xmlParse3986Authority -> xmlParse3986Host on it, with the host portion malformed enough that xmlParse3986Host returns ret != 0 and line 536 takes the true (return ret) side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry an intact 'http://[' prefix (e.g. id=29009d00708bb022 bytes 'http://[akeurl', id=0a912a4f189676dc 'http://[akeun'); the byte-diff shows W concentrating ':' '/' '[' at offsets 0x05-0x09 where '[' opens an authority/host that drives xmlParse3986Host to a nonzero return.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds corrupt the scheme delimiter bytes (e.g. id=0023aedf54362876 starts '01 00 70 3a 2f 2f' with the leading 'htt' destroyed, id=00bc240c762794a6 '00 00 80 74 70 3a 2f 2f') so the parser reaches xmlParse3986Host on a path that returns ret==0 (false side) - L hits line 536 with T=0 F=375.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer where the strcmp/memcmp interception logged the parser's literal scheme/delimiter operands, then substitutes those exact bytes into the input at the matching offsets, deterministically reconstructing the 'http://'-style prefix that naive's blind havoc mutators only stumble onto by chance.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of 'if (ret != 0) return(ret)' immediately after xmlParse3986Host is called.
xmlParse3986Authority is reached only when the caller sees '//' at the cursor.
Winning seeds contain an intact 'http://[' scheme-and-authority prefix.
Another winning seed also carries the http://[ prefix.
Winner bytes concentrate the ':' '/' '[' delimiter pattern at offsets 0x05-0x09 where loser bytes do not.
Loser-blocking seeds corrupt the leading scheme bytes before '://'.
On the loser, line 536 is reached only on its false side (ret==0), never the blocked true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 536  T=42 F=32  T=0 F=375  if (ret != 0) return(ret);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
**Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive resolves the branch at the same rate as cmplog once the 'http://' prefix is supplied in the seed corpus, showing the scheme delimiter is not what gates the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the true (ret != 0) side specifically requires a malformed host vs a well-formed one is inferred from L hitting only the false side; I cannot directly confirm from the prompt which host bytes make xmlParse3986Host return nonzero.
